title: Solid rose perfume balm
subtitle: A beeswax and jojoba balm scented with rose geranium essential oil
excerpt: A solid perfume balm made from beeswax, jojoba oil, and rose geranium essential oil. Applied with a fingertip to pulse points; wrists, neck, inside elbows. The beeswax releases the fragrance slowly as body heat melts the surface; jojoba oil's proximity to skin sebum helps the scent blend seamlessly. Anhydrous and preservative-free.
sourceNotes: Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), pp. 389-390, Pelargonium graveolens leave-on dermal limits. Solid perfume formulation notes, Bramble Berry (brambleberry.com, May 2026). IFRA standard 49, Category 1 (rinse-off body) and Category 4 (leave-on body).

recipe.yieldDescription: ~30 g solid perfume (fills one 30-60 ml salve tin)
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles to 60 g for two tins. Scale the essential oil at the same percentage.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Sets at room temperature in 30 minutes. Shelf life 12 months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed tin at room temperature. Anhydrous; no preservative needed. The jojoba oil (technically a wax ester) is very stable; the beeswax is indefinitely shelf-stable. The limiting ingredient is the rose geranium essential oil, which oxidises over 12-18 months at room temperature. Keep the tin lid on when not in use to slow oxidation.

glossaryTerms.definition: Contains no water. This balm is anhydrous and keeps without a preservative.

content.text: A solid rose perfume balm. The mechanics of a solid perfume differ from a liquid one: beeswax raises the melting point of the formula so the balm is solid at ambient temperature but melts instantly under the warmth of a fingertip. That brief melting phase releases the fragrance directly onto the skin, where the jojoba carrier (chemically a liquid wax very close in composition to skin sebum) holds the essential oil for an extended wear time. Rose geranium (Pelargonium graveolens) is a practical alternative to rose absolute; similar floral-rosy character at a fraction of the cost.

and requires no preservative. The rose geranium is at 6.5% of the total formula, applied to pulse points only; a matchhead-sized scrape is all that is needed. At this application amount, the skin exposure is well within IFRA guidelines for leave-on body products.

items.prepNote: cosmetic-grade beeswax pellets. 33% of the batch. Sets the balm solid at room temperature. Yellow beeswax has a gentle honey note that complements the floral EO.
clear or golden jojoba oil. 60% of the batch. Liquid wax ester; closely related to skin sebum; holds the fragrance on the skin.
Pelargonium graveolens rose geranium essential oil. 6.5% of the batch. Added off-heat at below 45°C to prevent flash-off. Apply this balm to pulse points only.

content.text: Place the beeswax pellets and jojoba oil in a small heatproof bowl over barely simmering water. Stir until the beeswax is fully melted and the blend is clear. Remove from the heat. Allow to cool to approximately 45°C; the blend will still be fully liquid but not too hot for the essential oil.
Add the essential oil and pour

Add the rose geranium essential oil and stir through quickly. Pour immediately into the salve tin before the beeswax starts to set; the blend solidifies rapidly once it drops below 45°C. A 30 g batch fills one 30-60 ml salve tin. Leave at room temperature for 30 minutes to set fully before putting the lid on.

Drag a clean fingertip across the surface of the balm to pick up a matchhead-sized amount. Apply to pulse points: inner wrists, side of the neck, inside elbows. The balm melts on contact with skin warmth and deposits a film of fragrance. A little goes a long way; start with one pulse point and build up on re-application if more scent is wanted.

Apply a small amount to the inside of the wrist and leave for 24-48 hours before regular use. Rose geranium essential oil can occasionally cause contact sensitivity in individuals with fragrance allergies. If redness or itching appears, discontinue use.

12 months at room temperature in a sealed tin. In warm weather above 28°C the balm may soften; store in a cool cupboard. The beeswax remains stable indefinitely; the rose geranium essential oil is the limiting factor and will lose complexity after 12-18 months.
